stem: A circular sector has radius \( [[r]] \) and central angle \( [[theta]]^{\circ} \). What is the area of the sector? (Express the answer in terms of \( \pi \).)
solution: A sector is the fraction \( \frac{[[theta]]}{360} \) of the full circle, whose area is \( \pi r^{2} \). So the sector area is \( \frac{[[theta]]}{360}\cdot\pi\cdot[[r]]^{2} = [[answer]] \).
distractor_rationales: This computes the arc length \( \frac{\theta}{360}\cdot 2\pi r \) instead of the sector area.
This is the area of the whole circle, ignoring the central-angle fraction.
This drops the factor of \( \pi \) from the circle's area.
This uses half the circle regardless of the actual central angle.
This is off from the correct area by \( \pi \).
This is off from the correct area by \( \pi \).
This doubles the correct sector area.

stem: An arc of a circle of radius \( [[r]] \) subtends a central angle of \( [[theta]]^{\circ} \). What is the length of the arc? (Express the answer in terms of \( \pi \).)
solution: An arc is the fraction \( \frac{[[theta]]}{360} \) of the full circumference \( 2\pi r \). So the arc length is \( \frac{[[theta]]}{360}\cdot 2\pi\cdot[[r]] = [[answer]] \).
distractor_rationales: This computes the sector area instead of the arc length.
This is the full circumference, ignoring the central-angle fraction.
This drops the factor of \( \pi \) entirely.
This uses half the circumference regardless of the actual central angle.
This is off from the correct arc length by \( \pi \).
This is off from the correct arc length by \( \pi \).
This is half the correct arc length.

stem: An arc of a circle of radius \( [[r]] \) subtends a central angle of \( \dfrac{[[num]]\pi}{[[den]]} \) radians. What is the length of the arc?
solution: In radians, arc length is simply \( s = r\theta \). Here \( \theta = \frac{[[num]]\pi}{[[den]]} \), so \( s = [[r]]\cdot\frac{[[num]]\pi}{[[den]]} = [[answer]] \).
distractor_rationales: This uses the sector-area formula \( \frac{1}{2}r^{2}\theta \) instead of the arc-length formula \( r\theta \).
This drops the radius entirely, using just the angle in radians.
This doubles the radius as if using a diameter instead of a radius.
This inverts the angle, swapping the numerator and denominator.
This is off from the correct arc length by \( \pi \).
This is half the correct arc length.

stem: A circular sector of radius \( [[r]] \) has area \( [[area_num]]\pi \). What is the measure, in degrees, of the sector's central angle?
solution: Since the area of a sector is \( \frac{\theta}{360}\pi r^{2} \), solving for \( \theta \) gives \( \theta = \frac{360\cdot[[area_num]]\pi}{\pi\cdot[[r]]^{2}} = [[answer]] \) degrees.
distractor_rationales: This uses \( 180 \) instead of \( 360 \) in the numerator, as if the sector were compared to a semicircle.
This just reports the area's coefficient instead of solving for the angle.
This forgets to square the radius in the denominator.
This multiplies by the radius instead of dividing by its square.
This is off from the correct value by ten degrees.
This is off from the correct value by ten degrees.

stem: An arc of a circle has length \( [[arclen_num]]\pi \) and subtends a central angle of \( [[theta]]^{\circ} \). What is the radius of the circle?
solution: Arc length is \( s = \frac{\theta}{360}\cdot 2\pi r \). Solving for \( r \): \( r = \dfrac{[[arclen_num]]\pi}{\frac{[[theta]]}{360}\cdot 2\pi} = [[answer]] \).
distractor_rationales: This omits the factor of 2 from the circumference, as if arc length were \( \frac{\theta}{360}\pi r \).
This just reports the arc length's coefficient of \( \pi \) instead of solving for the radius.
This multiplies by the angle instead of dividing it out.
This doubles the correct radius.
This is off from the correct radius by one.
This is off from the correct radius by one.

stem: A circular sector has radius \( [[r]] \) and central angle \( [[theta]]^{\circ} \). What is the perimeter of the sector (the two straight radii plus the curved arc)? (Express the answer in terms of \( \pi \).)
solution: The sector's boundary consists of two radii, each of length \( [[r]] \), plus the arc of length \( \frac{[[theta]]}{360}\cdot 2\pi[[r]] \). Adding these gives the perimeter \( 2\cdot[[r]] + \frac{[[theta]]}{360}\cdot 2\pi[[r]] = [[answer]] \).
distractor_rationales: This omits the two straight radii, giving only the arc length.
This mistakenly adds the sector's area instead of the arc length to the radii.
This includes only one radius instead of two.
This is just the full circumference, ignoring both the angle fraction and the two straight radii.
This is off from the correct perimeter by \( \pi \).
This is off from the correct perimeter by \( \pi \).
